name: multi-tenant-isolation-check
question: Does every domain-touching code path go through tenant-scoped repository methods? Are there raw SQL strings, methods accepting Connection outside the repository layer, or repository methods that take tenant_id as a non-TenantScope argument?

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <count> with file:line offenders on FAIL; exit code 0 on PASS, non-zero on FAIL

consumer_scope: P0+ all crates (every Stage 1+ worker; CI gate)
fallback: Read every .rs file under crates/ and grep for sqlx::query, &mut Connection, &mut PgConnection, &mut Transaction, Pool<Postgres>, pool.acquire( OUTSIDE crates/feedbackr-repository/. For repository methods: read every pub fn / pub async fn signature in crates/feedbackr-repository/src/**.rs and check first non-&self argument is &TenantScope or &ProjectScope (or allow-listed in allowlist.toml).

provenance.rationale: DEC-FBR-03 declares raw SQL outside repository layer a security incident. Type system (TenantScope/ProjectScope newtypes) is leg 1; this oracle is leg 2 (runtime/AST); clippy + cargo-deny is leg 3.
